# Argus

> Argus is a standalone search broker that routes queries across multiple web-search providers (SearXNG, Brave, Serper, Tavily, Exa) with automatic fallback, Reciprocal Rank Fusion ranking, health tracking, and budget enforcement. Connect via HTTP, CLI, MCP, or Python import. Add a provider key, it works. Remove it, it degrades gracefully.

## What It Does

> You pass Argus a search query. It fans out to multiple providers, collects results, ranks them via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF with k=60), deduplicates by URL, and returns a clean list. If a provider is down or over budget, it skips it automatically. Your project never touches a provider API directly.

> The output is designed for LLM consumption — enough context (title, snippet, URL, domain, relevance score) for a model to decide "this link is worth reading" without downloading the whole page.

## Installation

### Docker (recommended)

> cp .env.example .env and edit with your provider keys. docker compose up -d starts Argus + Postgres + SearXNG. Verify with curl http://localhost:8000/api/health.

### Local install

> git clone https://github.com/Khamel83/argus.git && cd argus; python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate; pip install -e ".[mcp]"; argus serve

## Provider Setup

> All you need is API keys for whichever providers you want. SearXNG is free and runs locally. The rest have generous free tiers.

> | Provider | Free tier | Get a key |
> |----------|----------|-----------|
> | SearXNG | Unlimited (self-hosted) | No key needed |
> | Brave Search | 2,000 queries/month | https://brave.com/search/api/ |
> | Serper | 2,500 queries/month | https://serper.dev |
> | Tavily | 1,000 queries/month | https://tavily.com |
> | Exa | 1,000 queries/month | https://exa.ai |

> Set keys in .env: ARGUS_BRAVE_API_KEY=, ARGUS_SERPER_API_KEY=, ARGUS_TAVILY_API_KEY=, ARGUS_EXA_API_KEY=. Unset or blank keys are silently skipped.

## Integration

### HTTP API

> POST /api/search with {"query": "...", "mode": "discovery", "max_results": 5}. POST /api/recover-url for dead URL recovery. GET /api/health/detail for provider status. GET /api/budgets for budget tracking. OpenAPI docs at /docs.

### CLI

> argus search -q "query" --mode discovery; argus search -q "query" --mode research -n 20; argus recover-url -u "https://dead.link"; argus health; argus budgets; argus test-provider -p brave

### MCP

> Start with `argus mcp serve` (stdio) or `argus mcp serve --transport sse --port 8001` (HTTP). Tools: search_web, recover_url, expand_links, search_health, search_budgets, test_provider. Resources: argus://providers/status, argus://providers/budgets, argus://policies/current.

> Claude Code config: {"mcpServers": {"argus": {"command": "argus", "args": ["mcp", "serve"]}}}
> Cursor config: same JSON in .cursor/mcp.json
> VS Code config: same JSON in settings.json under mcp.servers

### Python

> from argus.broker.router import create_broker; from argus.models import SearchQuery, SearchMode; broker = create_broker(); response = await broker.search(SearchQuery(query="...", mode=SearchMode.DISCOVERY, max_results=10)); results = response.results (each has url, title, snippet, domain, provider, score)

## Search Modes

> discovery: Find related pages and canonical sources. Chain: searxng → brave → exa → tavily → serper.
> recovery: Find a moved or dead URL. Chain: searxng → brave → serper → tavily → exa.
> grounding: Few live sources for fact-checking. Chain: brave → serper → searxng.
> research: Broad exploratory retrieval. Chain: tavily → exa → brave → serper.

## Configuration

> All config via environment variables with ARGUS_ prefix. Key variables: ARGUS_DB_URL (PostgreSQL), ARGUS_SEARXNG_BASE_URL, provider API keys, ARGUS_CACHE_TTL_HOURS (default 168), per-provider monthly budgets (ARGUS_BRAVE_MONTHLY_BUDGET_USD, etc.), ARGUS_RATE_LIMIT (default 60 req/min), ARGUS_BUDGET_DB_PATH (SQLite budget persistence).

## Architecture

> Caller (CLI/HTTP/MCP/Python) → SearchBroker → routing policy (per mode) → providers (parallel, with fallback) → cache → dedupe → RRF ranking → response. Provider adapters in argus/providers/ hide provider-specific logic. Broker in argus/broker/ owns routing, retries, budgets, degradation. API layer in argus/api/ is thin.

## Documentation

> README.md — full documentation with quick start, integration examples, and configuration reference
> docs/providers.md — provider setup, Docker networking, and configuration details
> CONTRIBUTING.md — contribution guide and how to add providers
> CLAUDE.md — developer guide and architecture overview
> docs/go-to-market.md — publicization strategy and timeline
